name: Northside Independent School District
districtFact: Largest school district in San Antonio and one of the fastest-growing districts in Texas, serving the north and northwest sides of the metro area.

about.title: About the District
about.content: Northside Independent School District is the largest school district in San Antonio and the fourth largest in Texas, serving approximately 103,000 students across 120 campuses.

planningTips: Battle of Flowers (April 23) is a no-school day specific to San Antonio and the Fiesta celebration — not observed in most other states. Mark it on your calendar early, especially if you have childcare to arrange.
Northside ISD Spring Break (Mar 8–12) is a week earlier than Dallas ISD and Austin ISD. Families coordinating with out-of-state relatives should confirm which district's break window to plan around.

Military families at Lackland AFB, Fort Sam Houston, or Randolph AFB: contact Northside ISD's enrollment office before your PCS arrival date. The district is experienced with expedited placement and can often place students within days.

San Antonio's pleasant March weather makes Spring Break ideal for local activities — River Walk, Natural Bridge Caverns, and Six Flags Fiesta Texas are popular but book up quickly once school breaks are announced.

districtFaqs.q: Is Northside ISD the same as UTSA (University of Texas at San Antonio)?
districtFaqs.a: No. Northside Independent School District (Northside ISD) is the K-12 public school system serving the north and northwest sides of San Antonio. The University of Texas at San Antonio (UTSA) is a separate public university with its own semester academic calendar. UTSA's schedule has no connection to Northside ISD school dates.

districtFaqs.q: What is the Battle of Flowers holiday in Northside ISD?
districtFaqs.a: The Battle of Flowers holiday (April 23) is a no-school day observed by Northside ISD and other Bexar County school districts in honor of San Antonio's Fiesta celebration. Fiesta San Antonio is a 10-day citywide festival dating back to 1891, and the Battle of Flowers parade is its most iconic event. The holiday is a Texas tradition largely unknown outside the region — families relocating from other states should add it to their calendar as it falls mid-week and may require childcare planning.

districtFaqs.q: How do Northside ISD and San Antonio ISD calendars compare?
districtFaqs.a: Northside ISD and San Antonio ISD are separate school districts serving different parts of the San Antonio metro area. Their academic calendars are generally similar — both start in early August and observe the same major holidays — but Spring Break dates and individual no-school days may differ by a day or two. Both districts observe the Battle of Flowers holiday. Families with children in multiple Bexar County districts should check each district's calendar independently.

districtFaqs.q: Does Northside ISD have support for military families?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Northside ISD serves the north side of San Antonio, which encompasses parts of the city near several Joint Base San Antonio (JBSA) installations, including Lackland Air Force Base and Fort Sam Houston. The district is experienced with mid-year PCS transfers and can often complete enrollment and school placement within days of a military family's arrival. Contact the Northside ISD enrollment office before your move date for the fastest placement process.

livingHere.intro: San Antonio is home to more military installations than any other city in the United States, and that military presence shapes Northside ISD's student population, enrollment patterns, and community calendar in ways that families from non-military areas may not anticipate. The city also hosts major tourism anchors — the River Walk, the Alamo, and Fiesta San Antonio — that affect local traffic and planning throughout the school year.

highlights.label: Five Military Installations
highlights.detail: Joint Base San Antonio encompasses Lackland AFB, Fort Sam Houston, and Randolph AFB, plus Camp Bullis and JBSA-Chapman. Military families account for a significant share of Northside ISD enrollment, meaning mid-year transfers due to PCS orders are routine — the district has a dedicated military liaison.

highlights.label: Battle of Flowers Holiday
highlights.detail: April 23 is a no-school day in Northside ISD and other Bexar County districts, tied to Fiesta San Antonio's Battle of Flowers parade. Fiesta is a 10-day citywide festival largely unknown outside Texas — families from out of state should add this mid-week holiday to childcare calendars in advance.

highlights.label: San Antonio International Airport
highlights.detail: SAT serves most major US hubs with Southwest, American, and United flights. Compared to DFW or IAH, it is a smaller airport with fewer nonstop options. Spring Break travel out of SAT is congested; flights to beach destinations and Mexico book out several weeks ahead.

highlights.label: River Walk & Tourism Traffic
highlights.detail: The San Antonio River Walk draws millions of visitors annually, peaking during Fiesta (April) and spring break. During these periods, central San Antonio traffic increases noticeably — families whose commute routes pass downtown should allow extra time in March and April.

highlights.label: Rapid Northwest Growth
highlights.detail: North and northwest San Antonio — the core of Northside ISD's territory — is among the fastest-growing residential corridors in Texas. New neighborhoods and elementary schools have been added repeatedly; attendance zone boundaries are updated regularly. Confirm your assigned school with the district before each school year.

highlights.label: Summer Heat & Drought
highlights.detail: San Antonio averages over 100°F on 10–15 days per year, typically in July and August. The Edwards Aquifer, which supplies the city's water, experiences drought-driven restrictions during dry summers — outdoor water use around the start of school may be limited by city ordinance.
